title: Reading and Use of English – Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between two and five words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

example.firstSentence: They sell fresh fish at the market every morning.

questions.firstSentence: It's not necessary to book in advance.
questions.explanation: Tests structures with 'have' (e.g. 'have to', causative, or perfect forms).

questions.firstSentence: The suitcase was so heavy that I couldn't lift it.
She succeeded in passing the driving test.
questions.explanation: Tests 'manage to' for achieving something with difficulty.
Tests phrasal verb 'give up' or passive with 'given'.

questions.firstSentence: Without his advice, I would have made the wrong choice.
questions.beforeGap: If he hadn't advised me, I

questions.firstSentence: She asked, 'What does this word mean?'
questions.explanation: Tests reported speech with an infinitive or question structure.
